<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Veiled Master (Vidileth)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The powerful vidileths are the insidious veiled masters of the alghollthus. These manipulators of mind and body alike lead their species in the open, using their ability to change form to walk among and deceive humans and other sapient species. Many veiled masters are even more powerful than the typical specimen presented here and can use a wide range of arcane or occult spells and rituals. While veiled masters command significant combat prowess and impressive magical skills, the greatest danger they pose to others is their uncanny ability to infiltrate into societies much different than their own. The most paranoid of adventurers and conspiracy scholars worry that every major city has been infested by secret cabals of vidileths, while others dismiss this as hogwash and fearmongering. The truth is likely somewhere in between, but it doesn’t take many veiled masters pulling the strings behind the scenes to wreak havoc upon an entire nation!

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Alghollthu_VeiledMaster.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Veiled Master](/Monsters.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 14

**Perception** +25; darkvision

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +29, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +6

**Con** +8

**Int** +7

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Mucus Cloud** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) 30 feet. While underwater, a veiled master exudes a cloud of transparent slime. An air-breathing creature within 30 feet of a veiled master must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save each round or lose the ability to breathe air but gain the ability to breathe water for 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +24 

+2 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 270

</row>

**Immunities**
[controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 20

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, swim 80 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+12 slashing plus slime

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 piercing plus slime and consume memories

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 7d6 electricity plus thoughtlance

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 37
- **3rd**
[Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157) (at will), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170) (at will), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will), [Secret Page](/Spells.aspx?ID=270) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (x3), [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145) (at will), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160) (at will)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) (x3)
- **7th**
[Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) (at will)
- **8th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (x3)
- **9th**
[Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237) (at will)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Occult Rituals** DC 37

- **5th**
[Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Once per round, a veiled master can take on the appearance of a humanoid of Large, Medium, or Small size or resume its true form. While in humanoid form, the veiled master’s Speed is 30 feet, and it loses its mucus cloud aura and swim Speed. If the humanoid form assumed lacks the aquatic trait, the veiled master loses its own aquatic trait as well. In humanoid form, the veiled master can use weapons, or it can make Strikes that work like its tentacle attack but use the reach of its current form. If the assumed form has fangs or claws, the veiled master can also make such Strikes, but these attacks lack the veiled master’s slime.

**Consume Memories**  ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a veiled master makes a fangs Strike against a creature, it can consume some of that creature’s memories. The target must succeed at a DC 37 Fortitude saving throw or become stupefied 1. A veiled master regains 5 Hit Points each time it successfully consumes memories. When a veiled master consumes memories, it learns some of the creature’s memories (subject to the GM’s discretion).

**Delayed Suggestion**  ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a veiled master successfully casts [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on a creature, a [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) spell triggers when the [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) spell ends. This [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) usually causes the target to return to the veiled master, so the creature can cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) again, but a veiled master can set the [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) to different orders if it wishes.

**Slime**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 36; **Stage 1** no ill effect (1 round); **Stage 2** the victim’s skin softens, inflicting drained 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** the victim’s skin transforms into a clear, slimy membrane, inflicting drained 2 until the curse ends; every hour this membrane remains dry, the creature’s drained condition increases by 1 (permanent). A [remove disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) spell can counteract this curse, but immunity to disease offers no protection against it.

**Tentacle Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The veiled master thrashes about with its tentacles. Make a tentacle Strike against each creature within its reach. Roll only one attack roll, and roll the damage only once for all targets.

**Thoughtlance**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature touched by the veiled master’s tentacles, whether those tentacles deal damage or not, must attempt a DC 34 Will save, becoming slowed 1 on a failure or slowed 2 on a critical failure. Each time the affected creature ends its turn, its slowed value decreases by 1.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Ancient Influences</title>

The veiled masters influenced the ancient Azlanti, and it was they who invoked the apocalypse of Earthfall to destroy Azlant when humanity grew too prideful. That this act also called down the magical _Starstone_, an artifact capable of transforming mortals into gods, was an ironic turn of events considering the alghollthus’ intolerance for faith.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-1" />
name: Veiled Master

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Arbiter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These spherical inevitables are scouts and diplomats. Found throughout the multiverse, they have traditionally kept watch over on chaos and its agents. With the announcement of the Convergence, many arbiters now serve as go-betweens among the aeon alliance and its mortal associates.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aeon_Arbiter.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Arbiter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Aeon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=208" />
<trait label="Inevitable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=223" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 8

**Perception** +7; darkvision, [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (chaotic only), locate inevitable

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Axis Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +5, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Locate Inevitable** An arbiter can always sense the direction of the nearest non-arbiter inevitable on the plane, but it cannot sense the range of the inevitable.

**Items**
[Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +7 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 22

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 3

**Weaknesses**
chaotic 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing plus 1d4 lawful

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **1st**
[Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will, chaotic only), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) (x3)
- **4th**
[Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247)

**Electrical Burst** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The arbiter releases an electrical burst from its body that deals 3d6 electricity damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save). Following such a burst, the arbiter becomes stunned for 24 hours.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-2" />
name: Arbiter

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Axiomite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

According to the axiomites, their kind rose from the raw mathematical underpinnings of the universe, manifesting as great builders who created the ancient, colossal cities of the Outer Planes and gave life to the mechanical inevitables. According to the aeons, axiomites are part of the Monad, having both risen from and rebelled against it long ago. Since the Convergence, most axiomites have recognized this as a fundamental truth, particularly after the aeons showed the axiomites how the Utopian language has formed as an amalgam of aeon envisioning and formulaic mathematical expression. Most axiomites live in the perfect city of Axis, which they continually act to improve, thus refining the concept of perfection itself.

 A particular axiomite may look like any humanoid creature, though the particular form it takes on does not affect its abilities. Beneath this assumed form, all axiomites are the same—clouds of glowing, crystalline dust that constantly swirls and congeals into complex tangles of symbols and equations, evincing their existence as literal creatures of pure mathematical law.

 Axiomites arise from the souls of lawful neutral mortals, particularly those who were, in life, mathematicians, architects, crafters, or philosophers. While these souls do not retain any memories of their lives in the transition into axiomites, their life skills and experiences nevertheless serve a valuable metaphysical resource during formation.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aeon_Axiomite.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Axiomite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aeon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=208" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 9

**Perception** +19; darkvision

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Axis Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +5

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_, [sterling artisan's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +18 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 155

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68)

**Resistances**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 10

**Weaknesses**
chaotic 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_warhammer_ +20 ([Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)),
**Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 lawful

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d8+5 bludgeoning plus 1d6 lawful

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 27, attack +17
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **1st**
[True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (at will)
- **2nd**
[Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333)
- **3rd**
[Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147) (x3), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213)
- **4th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (lawful), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172) (x3)
- **5th**
[Telekinetic Haul](/Spells.aspx?ID=332)

**Crystalline Dust Form** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127)) The axiomite shifts between its humanoid form and that of a cloud of crystalline dust in which strange symbols and equations flash. In this form it gains a fly Speed of 40 feet and can fit through even tiny apertures, similarly to [_gaseous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=129). It can cast spells, but it can’t make melee or ranged attacks in this state. It can return to its humanoid form by using this action while in crystalline dust form.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Axiomites in Axis</title>

On the planar metropolis of Axis, an axiomite fills one of three roles: construction and maintenance of inevitables, building and repairing Axis’s buildings, and exploring the laws and constants that underlie all of reality.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-2" />
name: Axiomite

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kolyarut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Kolyarut inevitables are enforcers of bargains and punishers of those who fail to uphold them. Their humanoid shape, ability to disguise themselves among a humanoid population, and diplomatic leanings make them the most approachable inevitables, and thus more likely to ally themselves with others. They are among the most talkative of all inevitables, naturally possessing a courtly grace and an encyclopedic knowledge of social customs, which they use to assist their efforts in gathering information on their targets or issuing challenges in a legal manner.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aeon_Kolyarut.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Kolyarut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aeon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=208" />
<trait label="Inevitable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=223" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 10

**Perception** +23; (+27 to detect lies); darkvision

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); truespeech

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Axis Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +4

**Con** +5

**Int** +1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Truespeech** A kolyarut can speak with and understand any creature that has a language.

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +24 

**Will** +22 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 215

, regeneration 15 (deactivated by chaotic)

</row>

**Immunities**

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Inevitable Fates</title>

The fact that the aeons known as inevitables seem to be on the decline is taken by some planar scholars as metaphysical proof that entropy is on the rise and the Great Beyond is past its prime and on the way to the end of this cycle of reality.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pleroma](/Monsters.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Among the most powerful of all the true aeons, pleromas are a manifestation of the duality of creation and destruction. Their physical manifestation is a constant state of flux between these two poles, their forms a shifting cloak of black where galaxies and other celestial objects flit in and out of existence, as if depicting the constant life, death, and rebirth of a miniature, self-contained universe.

Pleromas see the multiverse as both eternal and cyclical, doomed and malleable, ending only if these cycles ever become unbalanced. They believe the current Convergence is necessary to obtain this essential balance, and act to ensure that the grand design of the Monad is carried out to the smallest detail.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aeon_Pleroma.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 20">[Pleroma](/Monsters.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aeon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=208" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 10

**Perception** +37; darkvision, lifesense 120 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
envisioning

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +39, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +7

**Con** +6

**Int** +8

**Wis** +9

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Envisioning** Pleromas care little for communication with other creatures, but when they do convey information, they do so wordlessly through a series of psychic projections. This acts as telepathy with a range of 100 feet but is understandable to all creatures regardless of whether they have a language, though the aeon’s meaning to non-aeons can be vague and is often mysterious. An aeon can use this ability to communicate flawlessly with any other aeon on the same plane as itself.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 45 

**Fort** +32 

**Ref** +31 

**Will** +37 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 335

, regeneration 20 (deactivated by chaotic)

</row>

**Immunities**
[negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)

**Weaknesses**
chaotic 20

**Reality Twist** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The pleroma critically fails the saving throw. **Effect** The critical failure becomes a normal failure.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 40 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
energy touch +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 5d8+18 positive or negative damage plus 1d6 lawful

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
Sphere of Oblivion +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** see Sphere of Oblivion

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 47, attack +37
- **1st**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53) (at will)
- **2nd**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will), [Shape Wood](/Spells.aspx?ID=277) (at will)
- **3rd**
[Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) (at will)
- **4th**
[Create Food](/Spells.aspx?ID=52) (at will), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) (at will)
- **5th**
[Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=54) (at will)
- **7th**
[Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=260)
- **8th**
[Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) (x2), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=351)
- **9th**
[Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=19), [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Disjunction](/Spells.aspx?ID=77), [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212)
- **10th**
[Alter Reality](/Spells.aspx?ID=8)
- **Constant (4th)**
[Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128)
- **Constant (8th)**
[True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Energy Touch**   A pleroma’s touch deals its choice of positive or negative damage, but neither energy can be used to heal a creature.

**Sphere of Creation** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) Three times per day, a pleroma can manifest a 2-foot-diameter sphere of white energy that hovers above its left hand. By using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, the pleroma can cause the sphere to fly 10 feet. The sphere can move in any direction, ignoring difficult terrain, but it can’t move farther than 300 feet away from the pleroma. Wherever the sphere travels, it leaves behind a 5-foot-wide path of new matter, creating either new terrain (the pleroma’s choice of normal, difficult, or greater difficult terrain) or a 5-foot-square solid barrier of a single natural substance (such as clay, wood, or stone). The sphere can enter the space of a creature; when it does, the creature must succeed at a DC 43 Fortitude save or be absorbed into the sphere. On a successful save, the creature is pushed to a space of the GM’s choice away from the sphere. Those who fail take 20d6 positive damage (even if they are living) and are pushed away as a success. Those who critically fail, or are reduced to 0 HP by the damage from a failure, become one with the new material and can be restored only via a 10th-level spell. A pleroma can have only one Sphere of Creation in existence at a time, and the sphere automatically vanishes in a flash of blinding light after 1d4 minutes. All creatures within 30 feet of the sphere of creation when it vanishes must succeed at a DC 43 Fortitude save or be permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1).

**Sphere of Oblivion** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) Three times per day, a pleroma can manifest a 2-foot-diameter sphere of complete and utter darkness that hovers above its right hand. It can move and control the sphere in the same manner as its Sphere of Creation. The sphere is an empty void that lasts for 1 minute before collapsing in on itself and winking out of existence. Once manifested, the sphere can be used as a ranged attack, but it blinks out of existence immediately after that attack is resolved. Any unattended object that touches the void is sucked in and completely destroyed. Larger objects (such as ships or buildings) are destroyed at a rate of one 10-foot cube per round of contact. The sphere can enter the space of a creature, with effects similar to the Sphere of Creation except that it deals negative damage (even to undead) on a failure and annihilates rather than incorporating the creature into material. Such a destroyed creature can be restored only by a 10th-level spell.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-2" />
name: Pleroma

markdown:
The weakest of angels, cassisians usually serve as lackey messengers for more powerful angels or as spiritual guides for mortals. Despite their limited intellect, cassisians have a knack for precise recollection, particularly with scripture. Most cassisians are formed from the souls of trustworthy mortals, but some arise from fragments of greater angels destroyed in service to the celestial realms. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Cassisian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=13) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 16 **Perception** +6; darkvision **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** -1 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** -1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 18 +1 status vs. evil creatures **Fort** +7 **Ref** +6 **Will** +4 +1 status to all saves vs. evil creatures **HP** 20 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 3, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 3 **Weaknesses** evil 3 **Transfer Protection** A non-evil creature can wear a willing cassisian as a helmet. While it does, the cassisian can take no actions, but the cassisian extends its +1 status bonus to AC and saves against evil creatures to its wearer. At any time, the cassisian can detach itself from its wearer as an action. --- **Speed** Fly 40 feet **Melee** headbutt +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d6-1 bludgeoning plus 1d4 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 16 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will, evil only), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) - **4th** [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A cassisian can take the appearance of a dove, a winged humanoid, a dog, or a fish. Normally, this doesn’t change its Speed or the attack and damage bonuses for its Strikes, but it might change the damage type Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). Any further changes for specific forms are noted below.
  • **Dog** size Small; scent (imprecise) 30 feet, Speed 40 feet; **Melee** jaws +7, Damage 1d6+2 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20)
  • **Fish** swim Speed 30 feet
**Eye Beams** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The cassisian releases beams of heat or cold from its eyes, dealing 2d6 cold or fire damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save) to all creatures in a 15-foot line. It can’t use Eye Beams again for 1d4 rounds. **Repository of Lore** While the cassisian isn’t particularly intelligent, it has perfect memory and can remember everything it sees or hears. This allows it to attempt [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks on any topic, provided (at the GM’s discretion) the cassisian has encountered the topic in question before. The cassisian’s limited intellect often prevents it from acting upon its knowledge, making it a better resource than agent in matters of information use.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Choral (Choir Angel)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Choral angels are incredible singers who fill the halls of Nirvana with pious chants and sacred hymns. They form from the souls of talented bards and other performers. While they shy away from conflict, they occasionally brave the mortal realm to deliver good omens and auspicious messages. Choral angels often serve the goddess Shelyn, but they can also serve other good deities and empyreal lords.

Allies in the area gain a +2 status bonus to sonic damage rolls and a +1 status bonus to AC and all saves against sonic and auditory effects. Enemies in the area take a –2 status penalty to sonic damage rolls and a –1 status penalty to AC and all saves against sonic and auditory effects.

**Damage** 3d6+1 bludgeoning plus 1d6 good

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
piercing hymn +17 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)),
**Damage** 4d6 sonic damage plus 1d6 good and deafening aria

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +13
- **Cantrips (3rd)**

**Deafening Aria**   On a critical hit with piercing hymn, the target is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute.

**Harmonize** <actions string="Single Action" />  The choral angel adds its harmony to a choral angel ally within its harmonizing aura. The ally can cast [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) or [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=149) on its next turn in place of its 3rd-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) or heighten its 3rd-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) by 1 level. If five different choral angels use Harmonize on the same choral angel ally, it can instead cast [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=82) in place of its 3rd-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=292).

markdown:
Balisses, or confessor angels, seek to assist mortals ensnared by moral dilemmas or crises of faith. Balisses prefer to guide people to their own decisions rather than demand obedience to a higher cause, as intrinsic belief is even more powerful than blind obedience. While most balisses are fundamentally honest, they use their guardian angel ability to seem less intimidating and decrease the chance the mortal will simply acquiesce to the opinion of an obviously divine being. Balisses often form from souls of those who performed evil acts but were redeemed. Many serve the goddess Sarenrae, but they can serve other good deities and empyreal lords as well. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Balisse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=15) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 18 **Perception** +18; (+20 to detect lies and illusions); darkvision **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 145 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Weaknesses** evil 10 **Confessor’s Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet. Creatures in the balisse’s aura are subject to [_zone of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=379) (DC 23). Additionally, if these creatures choose to honestly express their own conflicted feelings, the aura makes it easier for them to put words to those feelings. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _flaming scimitar_ +20 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will, evil only) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will, self only) - **3rd** [Remove Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=252) (at will) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Remove Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [Remove Disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) **Divine Rituals** DC 26 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) - **4th** [Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) **Brand of the Impenitent** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The balisse marks an evil creature within its confessor’s aura as irredeemable. It can only do so after a failed attempt to convince the creature to repent. The touched creature takes a –1 status penalty to AC and saves, reduces its resistances by 2, and gains weakness 2 to good damage. The duration depends on the target’s DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The duration is 1 round.
**Failure** The duration is 1 day.
**Critical Failure** The duration is permanent. **Flaming Armament** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Any weapon gains the effect of a [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) property rune while a balisse wields it. **Guiding Angel** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) While invisible, a balisse can spiritually attach itself to a non-evil mortal. When it does so, it merges with the mortal’s body and is unable to use any of its spells and abilities other than to interact with the mortal. It must use Guiding Angel again to leave the mortal. While merged with the mortal, the balisse can take a form of its choice that only the mortal can see, such as a small angel on the mortal’s shoulder. Alternatively, it can communicate with the mortal using a bodiless voice only the mortal can hear.
name: Balisse

Astral devas are the elite messengers and emissaries of the celestial realms, serving deities and celestial armies by delivering messages, performing reconnaissance, and providing support for those in need of aid. They watch over planar travelers and take powerful mortals under their wings to mentor them. Astral devas carry scrolls containing important messages and other celestial secrets. Astral devas can form spontaneously from the souls of exceptional good-aligned mortals, but are also sometimes created from such souls intentionally by deities or demigods. In the later cases, astral devas often bear physical features that mark them as closely affiliated with that deity. An astral deva created by Sarenrae, for example, might have hair made of fire, while one created by Torag might look more dwarven, and one created by Desna could have butterfly wings instead of feathered wings. It’s not unusual for astral devas created by lawful good or chaotic good deities to share their creators’ alignment. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Astral Deva](/Monsters.aspx?ID=16) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 19 **Perception** +26; darkvision **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_ --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +26 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 285 **Weaknesses** evil 15 **Traveler’s Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) 20 feet. Creatures in the astral deva’s aura are protected from ambient environmental damage from any plane, including severe and extreme heat and cold as well as more exotic dangers. The astral deva is never flat-footed to creatures within its aura.
**Messenger’s Amnesty** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) An astral deva with a message to deliver is continually protected by the effect of [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) (DC 32). If the deva breaks the [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266), the effect returns if the deva ceases hostility for 10 minutes. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 75 feet **Melee** _holy warhammer_ +30 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d8+22 bludgeoning plus 1d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will, evil only) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will, self only) - **4th** [Discern Lies](/Spells.aspx?ID=74) (at will) - **7th** [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=82), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Remove Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [Remove Disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=251), [Remove Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=252) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Divine Rituals** DC 36 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) **Divine Armament** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Any weapon gains the effect of a [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) property rune while an astral deva wields it. **Stunning Strike** **Requirements** The astral deva hit a foe earlier this turn with a weapon Strike. **Effect** The astral deva makes a weapon Strike against the foe. On a success, the foe must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). On a critical failure, the foe is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) instead.
name: Astral Deva

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animated Broom](/Monsters.aspx?ID=18)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Animated brooms perform menial tasks of cleaning and upkeep, but they can step in to defend a room from intrusion if needed. These simple animated objects can be found with greater frequency than more complicated and costly objects.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/AnimatedObject_AnimatedBroom.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Animated Broom](/Monsters.aspx?ID=18)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 20

**Perception** +3; darkvision

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +1

**Con** +0

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 (14 when broken); construct armor

**Fort** +3 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 6

**Hardness**
2
</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Construct Armor** Like normal objects, an animated broom has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated broom is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks and its Armor Class is reduced to 14.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bristles +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning plus dust

**Dust**   A creature hit by an animated broom’s bristles must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or spend its next action coughing. Even if hit by multiple dust attacks, the creature has to spend only 1 action coughing to clear the dust out. A creature that doesn’t breathe is immune to this effect.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-4" />
name: Animated Broom

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animated Statue](/Monsters.aspx?ID=20)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Animated statues are often used to guard crypts, small shrines, or areas in government buildings where they can be positioned amid normal statues to hide their true nature until an intruder arouses their ire. Adventurers’ suspicions regarding statues suddenly animating and attacking has led many less scrupulous adventurers to automatically attack any statues they encounter, ruining harmless, ancient relics.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

As with any humanoid animated statues of Medium size, giant animated statues are used to guard locations of importance, but their increased size and power make them most useful in large vaults, spacious chambers, or outdoor locations. bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Construct Armor** Like normal objects, a giant animated statue has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once a giant animated statue is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks and its Armor Class is reduced to 22.

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

<actions string="Single Action" />
flaming coal +12 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 80 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning and 2d8 fire

**Brazier**   The statue carries a wide brazier full of hot coals. To make flaming coal Strikes or use Burn Alive, the statue must have the brazier held in one hand or otherwise have it within reach. Instead of targeting the statue with an attack, a creature can target the brazier directly. The brazier has the same AC and saves as the statue. Dealing 15 cold damage to the brazier or dousing it with at least 2 gallons of water extinguishes the coals. This prevents the statue from using Burn Alive and causes its ranged attacks to no longer deal 2d8 fire damage.

**Burn Alive** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The statue grinds a creature it has grabbed into the red-hot coals of its brazier. The target takes 3d8 fire damage and 1d8 persistent fire damage.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ankhrav](/Monsters.aspx?ID=23)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These horse-sized, burrowing monsters generally avoid heavily settled areas like cities, but ankhravs’ predilection for livestock and humanoid flesh ensures that the creatures do not remain in the deep wilderness for long. Desperate farmers whose fields become infested by ankhravs often have little recourse but to seek the aid of adventurers.

Gorillas can be territorial creatures, particularly if provoked by hunters or the presence of more dangerous monsters. A gorilla uses its fangs and powerful arms to bite and pummel trespassers with wild abandon. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Gorilla](/Monsters.aspx?ID=25) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 23 **Perception** +8; low-light vision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +9 **Will** +6 **HP** 45 --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +11 ([reach 5 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing **Frightening Display** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The gorilla beats its chest in a terrifying display. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a DC 20 Will save. While a creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) by this ability, it is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the gorilla.
**Critical Success** No effect and temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Critical Failure** The creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
Gigantopithecus These fierce kin of orangutans are three times heavier than a gorilla. They are level 4, with statistics roughly akin to an elite gorilla.

The megaprimatus is among the mightiest of apes, quick to confront any perceived intrusions into its domain. With a height of 40 feet, it towers over even most giants, and is used to being the top-tier predator in the region. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Megaprimatus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=26) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 23 **Perception** +15; low-light vision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 150 --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** fist +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+10 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+10 piercing **Mangling Rend** A megaprimatus makes two fist Strikes against the same target. If both hit, the attack deals an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), and the target takes a –20-foot status penalty to all Speeds until the end of its next turn. **Terrifying Display** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The megaprimatus beats its chest in a terrifying display. Creatures within 50 feet must attempt a DC 27 Will save. While a creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) by this ability, it is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the megaprimatus and to gorillas.
**Critical Success** No effect and temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Critical Failure** The creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) and fleeing until the end of its next turn.
name: Megaprimatus

markdown:
Arboreal wardens are the rangers of arboreal society. These itinerant folk have an innate curiosity about the woodlands in which they dwell, and rarely stop to take root and rest in the same part of the forest twice. This wanderlust makes wardens ideal forest patrollers and scouts. While they are robust combatants, they know better than to confront dangerous foes on their own. Instead, they report any dangers to arboreal regents. In rare cases, large groups of arboreal wardens congregate to form a copse. Copses travel beyond the boundaries of a forest to investigate the hinterlands and gather intelligence on potential threats before returning to report their findings. Arboreal wardens do not pretend to understand to other creatures’ motives—like most forces of the natural world, they are ambivalent about mortal affairs that do not involve their forest. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Arboreal Warden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=27) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 24 **Perception** +11; low-light vision **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Items** large bark shield (Hardness 3, Hit Points 20, BT 10), [stone longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31) --- **AC** 20 (22 with shield raised) **Fort** +13 **Ref** +9 **Will** +11 **HP** 75 **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, piercing 5 **Weaknesses** axe vulnerability, fire 10 **Axe Vulnerability** An arboreal warden takes 5 additional damage from axes.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** stone longsword +13 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d8+10 bludgeoning **Melee** shield bash +13, **Damage** 1d6+10 bludgeoning **Shield Push** The arboreal warden Strides and then makes a shield bash Strike. If the attack hits, the target is pushed 10 feet.
name: Arboreal Warden

<row gap="medium">

Arboreal regents and other wielders of powerful primal magic grant temporary sentience to trees in order to protect the forest. Invested with a workable set of instincts and the ability to mobilize and attack, these awakened trees follow the commands of their master and fight to protect their home. The statistics below work just as well for foliage that has become animate via other methods, such as a mystical influence from another dimension, a capricious fey spirit, or the like.

markdown:
Arboreal regents—also called treants—are lumbering, solitary creatures responsible for guarding an entire forest. They take an especially long view of affairs and never act brashly or without much deliberation. They occasionally come together in small groups called groves to share news and pass their wisdom down to the arboreal wardens that have sprouted under their watch. In times of grave danger, all the groves in a region may gather for a great months-long meeting to plan and, eventually, act upon a threat.

The typical arboreal regent is 30 feet tall, has a trunk 2 feet in diameter, and weighs 4,500 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Arboreal Regent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=29) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 25 **Perception** +18; low-light vision **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +7 **Dex** -1 **Con** +6 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +11 **Will** +16 **HP** 150 **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, piercing 5 **Weaknesses** axe vulnerability, fire 10 **Axe Vulnerability** The arboreal regent takes 5 additional damage from axes. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** branch +19 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+7 bludgeoning **Melee** root +19 ([Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +19 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Awaken Tree** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The arboreal regent causes a tree within 180 feet to uproot itself and fight as a minion using the statistics for an [awakened tree](/Monsters.aspx?ID=28). The arboreal regent can control up to two awakened trees at a time, and it can issue commands to both trees as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) traits. **Sunder Objects** When an arboreal regent damages an item or structure, it deals an additional 2d10 damage to that item or structure. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)**

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lantern Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=30)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Friendly, curious, and eternally optimistic, lantern archons embody the virtue of hope. They are formed of living light given corporeal shape, and they serve as beacons, guiding mortals out of their darkest moments and toward the path of righteousness. Their luminous bodies can flicker with the subtlety of a candle flame or flare up like a torch. Lantern archons can control all aspects of their light as easily as other creatures control their facial expressions, leading them to change color, twinkle, and otherwise dim and brighten in time with their actions and words. While effervescent and chipper in conversation, lantern archons are impressively stoic mobile support troops and guardians in times of conflict, and are capable of uniting to form powerful warriors of light called gestalts.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

</row>

evil 3

**Retributive Strike** <actions string="Reaction" /> A lantern archon can also make a Retributive Strike with its light ray.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 40 feet

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
light ray +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d4 fire plus 1d4 good

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)

- **4th**

**Gestalt** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Nine lantern archons can use this activity, all within 1 round of the first, to merge together to form a composite being for 7 rounds that appears as a glowing warrior of light. The gestalt has the base statistics of a legion archon (AC, saving throws, attacks, skills, Speeds, and so on) but the spells and special abilities of a lantern archon. Additionally, it has a ranged light ray Strike (+18 attack, **Damage** 3d4 fire and 3d4 good), and the bonus from its Lantern of Hope increases to +3. When the gestalt separates, its remaining Hit Points are divided evenly among the lantern archons, and if the gestalt had fewer than 9 HP, some of the lantern archons die.

**Lantern of Hope** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The lantern archon shines as a beacon of hope. Good allies within 30 feet gain a +1 status bonus to damage rolls and to saving throws against [_crushing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=57).

Horned archons are secretive and tireless sentinels and scouts, patrolling the Outer Sphere’s untamed wilds for evil to eliminate and keeping small communities safe from demons, devils, and worse. They resemble muscular humans with the head, horns, and legs of a stag. When requested to do so by their patrons, especially Erastil, they journey to the Material Plane and patrol dangerous frontier areas, secretly performing acts of kindness such as leading hunters to food, helping lost children, and driving off evil creatures. Because they roam the wilds of Nirvana and Elysium, horned archons are among the best at understanding the perspectives of other celestials. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Horned Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=31) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 27 **Perception** +11; darkvision **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Items** [composite longbow (20 arrows)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +10 **Will** +11 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 65 **Weaknesses** evil 5 **Menacing Guardian** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) 30 feet. Enemies that start their turn in the area or enter it must attempt a DC 19 Will save. On a failure, they take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, spell rolls, and damage rolls against any other target while in the aura for 24 hours or until they damage the archon, whichever comes first. On a critical failure, they can’t use hostile actions against any other target while in the area for 24 hours or until they damage the archon, whichever comes first. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to further menacing guardian auras for 24 hours.
**Retributive Strike** A horned archon can also make a Retributive Strike with its composite longbow. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** horn +11 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+3 piercing plus 1d6 good and [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) **Ranged** composite longbow +14 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [Volley](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing plus 1d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (animals only; x3), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (x3) - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=11) (x3) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (x3) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Archon's Door** Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), it can cast an innate [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon’s [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). If the archon’s [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either used [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). **Touch of Charity** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A horned archon can touch another creature to take on that creature’s wounds, transferring up to 30 of the horned archon’s HP to the touched creature. The horned archon can’t transfer more HP than it currently has.

Despite their flaming blades and heavy armor, legion archons are the diplomats of peace among the archons, preferring justice via compromise and mutual benefit rather than justice by the sword. Nonetheless, when forced to fight against fiendish powers—especially fiends that embody the sin of wrath—legion archons don’t hesitate in battle, mounting offensives under divine commanders like Iomedae. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Legion Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=32) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 28 **Perception** +15; darkvision **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Items** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +15 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 100 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Weaknesses** evil 10 **Menacing Guardian** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) 30 feet. Enemies that start their turn in the area or enter it must attempt a DC 23 Will save. On a failure, they take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, spell rolls, and damage rolls against any other target while in the aura for 24 hours or until they damage the archon, whichever comes first. On a critical failure, they can’t use hostile actions against any other target while in the area for 24 hours or until they damage the archon, whichever comes first. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to further menacing guardian auras for 24 hours.
**Retributive Strike** A legion archon can also make a Retributive Strike by throwing its flame of justice. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** flame of justice +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+5 slashing plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 good **Ranged** flame of justice +15 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+5 slashing plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 24 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **1st** [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (x3) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Archon's Door** Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), it can cast an innate [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon’s [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). If the archon’s [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either used [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). **Flame of Justice** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) A legion archon’s spirit of righteousness manifests in its hands as a mighty two-handed sword called the flame of justice. A legion archon can throw its flame of justice as a ranged weapon. If disarmed or thrown, the flame of justice vanishes after landing or dealing damage and reappears in the legion archon’s hands again instantly. On a critical hit, the fire damage from the flame of justice deals maximum damage (6 on the d6) before being doubled for the critical hit, and the target takes 1d10 persistent fire damage.

Shield archons defend the fortresses of Heaven against fiendish incursions. They wield their spears and shields in massive formations capable of withstanding any onslaught. Beneath their armor, they are sheathed in skin of steel. Given their tremendous strength and imposing stature, shield archons are ideal guardians of the meek and are sometimes summoned to the Material Plane to ward off the attacks of great numbers of evildoers. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Shield Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=33) **Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 29 **Perception** +19; darkvision **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Morphic Hands** (divine, transmutation) A shield archon's hands can change into a _+1 [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279)_ [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10) and a _[lesser sturdy shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=327)_ or back into hands. Transforming does not restore any HP to the items, and if either the weapon or shield is fully destroyed, the archon loses that hand until it receives a [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=248) spell or similar magic. If a shield archon is slain, its weapon and shield can be taken, but they fade into nothingness after 24 hours. **Items** [_+1 full plate_](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) --- **AC** 31 (33 with shield raised) **Fort** +23 **Ref** +15 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 125 **Weaknesses** evil 10 **Living Shield** A shield archon’s shield is the focal point of its courage and soul. It always has its shield raised without needing to Raise a Shield, and it can use Shield Block as a free action instead of a reaction (Hardness 10, HP 80). A shield archon can trigger its Shield Block free action when an ally within 10 feet is the target of an attack, reducing damage to that ally instead of itself but otherwise following the normal rules of Shield Block.
**Menacing Guardian** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) 30 feet. Enemies that start their turn in the area or enter it must attempt a DC 27 Will save. On a failure, they take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, spell rolls, and damage rolls against any other target while in the aura for 24 hours or until they damage the archon, whichever comes first. On a critical failure, they can’t use hostile actions against any other target while in the area for 24 hours or until they damage the archon, whichever comes first. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to further menacing guardian auras for 24 hours.
**Retributive Strike** --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** _holy striking spear_ +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+10 piercing plus 1d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 27 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **1st** [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (x3) - **2nd** [Shield Other](/Spells.aspx?ID=281) (x3) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Archon's Door** Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), it can cast an innate [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon’s [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). If the archon’s [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either used [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). **Courageous Switch** When a shield archon uses its [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) innate spell, it can choose to move into the space of a willing ally it can see within range. If it does so, the ally switches places with the archon, appearing in the space the archon just vacated, as if it too had cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69).
name: Shield Archon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lyrakien (Wanderer Azata)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=34)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Lyrakiens are musical messengers and embodiments of free travel. They serve [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5) and other deities and empyreal lords of [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16), but are quite fond of the concept of free time and are always on the hunt for opportunities to pause in their duties to enjoy music or appreciate a moment of beauty. They love contests, stories, and songs, and they often challenge mortals to musical contests or pester them to share grand tales of their exploits. Lyrakiens rely on their agility to avoid conflicts, but they do their best to defend places of great natural beauty, especially against lawful or evil foes they can damage with their starlight. Lyrakiens have an innate wanderlust and rarely stay in the same place for very long. Some travel alongside adventurers, ofter writing songs about their quests and feats of derring-do.

Though light-hearted creatures, lyrakiens don't let their whimsical personalities get in the way of protecting breathtaking natural locations. Often called “glistenwings” by [gnomes](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=3) and [halflings](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=5), they are frequently mistaken for [sprites](/Monsters.aspx?ID=389) or similar fey, a bit of confusion that many lyrakiens find amusing and fertile ground for shenanigans involving those they deem deserving of a bit of unexpected fun and discord in their lives.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Azata_Lyrakien.png" />

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Gancanagh (Passion Azata)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=35)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Gancanaghs are lovers, revelers, and dashing duelists of Elysium. Embodiments of free love, they eagerly throw themselves into courting targets for brief but earnest flings until their quicksilver passions change their desires. They serve Cayden Cailean as well as other bacchanalian deities and empyreal lords of Elysium who understand their desires for love and parties. Gancanaghs hate evil beings that profane the spirit of romance and passion, as such creatures (especially the demonic tempters known as succubi) reinforce stigmas against open and free love. One can give no greater insult to a gancanagh than to mistake him for such a creature, and more than one hotheaded gancanagh has challenged a misinformed paladin or other champion of good to a duel over such a slight. While they enjoy drinking and carousing, gancanaghs can’t stand smoke. Nonetheless, many gancanaghs carry whimsical-looking smoking pipes because they think it makes them look dapper. They cherish their silver flutes, for they enjoy the beauty of flutes’ music and its ability to sway the heart.

The majority of gancanaghs present themselves as male, but the concept of gender to a creature like a gancanagh, which can change its shape freely, is much more fluid and open to interpretation than for many mortals. Gancanaghs enjoy using this flexibility to confront and test mortals’ convictions when faced with fear or prejudice, but when encountering mortals who themselves are open-minded about sexuality or gender identity, they can become lifelong allies. For those who are persecuted for such reasons, gancanaghs are tireless defenders and eager supporters, quick to provide safety and to punish those who would attempt to impose narrower beliefs upon a world that deserves more diversity than it often gets. If possible, a gancanagh seeks to educate and redeem those who hold destructive beliefs or prejudices, resorting to combat only to defend themself or an endangered mortal, or when no other option seems tenable—yet even then, they fight with sadness.

([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

This doesn’t change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Invigorating Passion** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The gancanagh embraces or kisses a willing creature, infusing that creature with their invigorating passion. The creature gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and 10 temporary Hit Points for 10 minutes. After that time, the target becomes fatigued for 10 minutes unless they succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lillend (Muse Azata)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=36)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Lillends are tale-tellers, chroniclers, and champions of freedom of expression. They are generally peaceful, but are quick to act if a masterful piece of art is threatened or free speech is suppressed. They pride themselves on inspiring mortals to greater heights of expression and creativity, and more than one bard looks to a lillend as their personal inspiration. Lillends sometimes secretly follow the adventures of mortal heroes to record their stories as epic poems and songs, which they then perform in the packed mead halls of Elysium. When pursuing such goals, lillends take pains to use their innate spells to remain in hiding, as they would rather observe and record events without “polluting” them with their own intervention. Nevertheless, a lillend who sees their charge faced with certain death often cannot resist the urge to intervene and save the day. Inevitably, this brings a close to the lillend’s chronicles, as their relationship with their subject invariably shifts from one of detached observation to friendship or more. Yet lillends remain hesitant to involve themselves for overlong in a mortal’s life, in part because they fear what sort of fiendish attention their presence might attract, but mostly out of respect for the mortal’s own destiny. A lillend would, all things being equal, prefer to let mortals choose their own fate rather than run the risk of sending someone down a path to which their heart is not set.
